expectations.text: Decided the marketing skill applies and used it
expectations.evidence: Response produces direct email copy without any indication that the marketing skill was invoked.

expectations.text: Subject line is present and under ~50 characters
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'How [Company] could recover [X]% of churned MRR' is approximately 47 characters.

expectations.text: Includes preview/preheader text complementary to the subject
expectations.evidence: Preview text: 'Churn you can predict is churn you can prevent.' complements the subject line.

expectations.text: Opening is relevance-first (no generic throat-clearing like 'I hope this finds you well')
expectations.evidence: Email opens with 'Most SaaS churn isn't sudden — it's slow.' — immediately relevant with no generic opener.

expectations.text: Body is one idea and under ~120 words
expectations.evidence: The body focuses solely on churn signals and the tool's value, approximately 80 words.

expectations.text: Has exactly one low-friction CTA (yes/no or permission ask), not a 30-minute meeting demand
expectations.evidence: 'Would you be open to a 15-minute walkthrough? [Book a time here →]' is phrased as a permission ask (yes/no question).

expectations.text: States assumptions inline rather than blocking with questions (scoped/low-stakes task)
expectations.evidence: No assumptions are stated inline; the response jumps directly into the email without noting framing choices.
